---
title: "TransformModel"
generated: true
description: "Chart between Lie parameters and a homogeneous transform."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.register`](../nitrix-register.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.register._model`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TransformModel -->
```python
class TransformModel(Protocol)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:TransformModel -->
Chart between Lie parameters and a homogeneous transform.

An implementer exposes the exponential map (parameters -> matrix),
the parameter count for a given spatial rank, and the inter-level
translation rescale the coarse-to-fine driver applies when it
warm-starts a finer pyramid level.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TransformModel.n_params -->
```python
n_params(ndim: int) -> int
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Length of the parameter vector for an `ndim`-D transform.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TransformModel.exp -->
```python
exp(params: Float[Array, '... p'], *, ndim: int) -> Float[Array, '... d1 d1']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Exponential map from parameters to a homogeneous matrix.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TransformModel.rescale_to_grid -->
```python
rescale_to_grid(params: Float[Array, ' p'], ratio: Float[Array, ' d']) -> Float[Array, ' p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Rescale the parameters from one voxel grid to a finer one.

Used by the coarse-to-fine driver when it warm-starts a finer
pyramid level: the returned parameters reproduce the same
physical transform on the finer grid, since translations are
expressed in voxel units and so scale with the grid.


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:TransformModel -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
